supervisedQueue.change: Split the areaCoverage multiplier OUT of the matched-quality channels in perelement-score.mjs (L827-834): report RAW matched color/typo/text/pos as the FIDELITY channel, and areaCoverage as a SEPARATE completeness channel; re-weight independently in grade-sections.mjs (L331-338) instead of letting coverage multiply quality to near-zero. Un-conflates completeness from matched quality so a color-perfect clone stops reading 0.21 color. ALSO FIX band-misalignment: map source bands to clone bands by content/anchor (or hRatio-normalize) before per-band scoring, so present-but-shifted text is not flagged missing (penalizes reflow). Both are false-deflation arms. CONCLUSIVE (2 structures failed anti-gaming): the honest fix is RASTERIZED-AREA ATTRIBUTION — credit source area covered by a clone region-capture raster as covered (not missing) when computing areaCoverage, so coverage distinguishes rasterized-present (good) from dropped (incomplete). Editability dim already penalizes raster, so crediting raster for visual-coverage is the right separation. STATUS: rasterized-area attribution is the right anti-gaming-correct approach (proven: incomplete clone gets 0 raster credit). It is ONE LINE from working — perelement-score.mjs RASTERCOV_MIN_AREA_FRAC gate must compare band area to VIEWPORT height (refW*900), not the full-page-height normalizer refHscaled. Finish that line + re-run the A/B to ship a ready-to-flip honest grader. ALSO (editability arm): grade-structure.mjs FALSE-INFLATES editability by crediting nominal __globals__ presence even when inline styles override them (globalsCoverageEffective=0 vs nominal 0.91). Add an inline-override-effectiveness check so editability reflects whether globals actually propagate. The grader lies BOTH ways.

flexGrowPortability.fact: build-flow/build-absolute emit _flex_grow on text-editor widgets. Elementor 4.0.9 (sg-host, real corpus stack) ACCEPTS it; Elementor 3.28.4 (localhost) REJECTS the WHOLE save with 422 schema.invalid_settings (text-editor has no control named _flex_grow) -> blank page. flexGrowPortability.impact: Not blocking the flywheel (corpus = sg-host 4.0.9). BUT a real product-portability bug: on any Elementor 3.28.x customer site every build would produce a blank page. Queue for product hardening: version-probe + omit/guard 4.x-only controls on older Elementor.

responsiveFrontierTruth.flowSolved: build-flow ALREADY reflows multi-card/logo grids 3->2->1 via emitted grid_columns_grid_tablet + grid_columns_grid_mobile per-breakpoint overrides (verified clean single-column at 390, zero horizontal overflow on tailwind+vercel+supabase).

responsiveFrontierTruth.openWall: The responsive gap is the ABS builder: recipe #20 abs-responsive-unpin does only a CRUDE everything->1col collapse at narrow widths (no 3->2->1 card-row regrouping). ABS is the router primary (0.648 vs flow 0.616), so its responsive term caps the router. LEVER: detect card/comparable-width rows in the abs tree + apply flows per-breakpoint grid-override reflow at tablet/mobile instead of the blanket 1col un-pin.

absMobileOverflowBug.bug: build-absolute pins the editable full-width header <nav> + <footer> chrome at the SOURCE css width:1440px and NEVER un-pins them responsively. Result: docW=1440 at the 390 viewport on EVERY abs clone -> horizontal scroll + content clipped off the left edge at mobile. A glaring, human-obvious mobile defect on all absolute clones; it (not the card-rows) is why the abs-responsive port measured reflows=false.
absMobileOverflowBug.fix: un-pin the nav/footer (and any fixed-width full-bleed chrome) to width:100% / responsive at <=1024 via the same scoped @media custom_css channel recipe #20 already uses for the body un-pin, OR emit them at width:100% from the start. Render test: docW MUST == viewport at 390 (no horizontal scroll); nav/footer full-width-responsive; desktop identical.
absMobileOverflowBug.leverage: HIGH + BROAD — affects mobile fidelity on EVERY abs clone (router primary). Raises the responsive baseline across the corpus. Independent of the card-row reflow. Likely-keep (clear bug, clear test). Also UNBLOCKS a clean re-test of the grid card-row reflow port next.

graderResponsiveSelftestFlaky.status: FIXED — SELFTEST clone = deepcopy of source (deterministic EXACTLY 1.0 x5).

Human-visible mobile fidelity regression; the grader (mobile-prop measures docH/overflow, SSIM is desktop) does NOT catch it.

absMobileBgVanishRegression.status: known — fix queued (separate round; bg-correctness, human-visible, ungraded)

absMobileResidualTruth: After vreflow #23: mobile-docH residual is DOMINANTLY un-pinned IMAGES rendering full-width at natural aspect (framer 46%/supabase 27%), then retained text heights (~20%), 12px reflow margins (9-13%), card-row grid heights. Image mobile-width constraint (keep captured width) is the biggest lever. This is the abs-builders fundamental desktop-pixel->mobile-stack limitation; full 1:1 mobile compaction is hard.

absMobileCeiling.finding: The abs-builder has an ARCHITECTURAL mobile ceiling: vreflow #23 crushed mobile docH from 2-9.5x to 0.95x(tailwind)/3.1x(supabase)/4.76x(framer), but the RESIDUAL (un-pinned images full-width at natural aspect = framer 46%, retained text heights ~20%, 12px reflow margins, 1-col image stacking vs source dense rows) is fundamental to flat desktop-pixel to mobile-stack. Further squeezing = diminishing returns + desktop-leak risk (vreflow-residual broke tailwind desktop). Full mobile 1:1 needs the FLOW builder or the structure-inference rework (wave-4 #2). DECISION: responsive given its 2-3 rounds (big keep #23 + thorough diagnosis); PIVOT to the #2 corpus lever = STRUCTURAL floor 0.4973 (dynamic-content-not-captured).

structuralGapTruth.finding: The struct floor (reactdev 0.34, resend 0.337, framer 0.383) is NOT click-gated content. It is: (a) canvas / iframe(Sandpack) / live-React-output PREVIEW surfaces that paint visually but have no/sparse walkable DOM -> dropped -> sparse failing sections; (b) decorative-SVG counted/missed as content. LEVER: surface region-raster (element-level image per cohesive visual surface, blank-raster-guarded, GPU flags for canvas) + SVG-vs-content classification. NOT click-to-reveal (proven dead).

surfaceRasterWordBuryingConcern.status: RESOLVED (recipe #26 word-safe tighten): gate counts the element's own text + bails on headings -> the hero H1 is native text again; text-free surfaces still raster.

threeRoundPlan.1: flow-router: re-baseline build-FLOW on the honest grader + best-per-site router (flow cracks responsive where abs is capped; linear flow 0.607 vs abs 0.378). Biggest UNCAPPED lever for the responsive floor.
threeRoundPlan.2: dynamic-content-not-captured (capture-side: imagery region-capture + structured-code-block capture) — broadest addressable, 4 sites, struct+visual.
threeRoundPlan.3: text-collision de-dupe in build-absolute (USER feedback #4, supabase hero overlap + nav/CTA dup; cheap flagship-restore).

flowRouterFinding.finding: Router (best-per-site abs vs flow) gains only ~+0.005 (flow wins 2/7: supabase, linear). Flow responsive 0.3129 > abs 0.2296 but flow visual/structural lower -> net flow<=abs. Do NOT adopt route-to-flow as a corpus lever. The responsive floor needs a hybrid (abs fidelity + flow reflow) or structure-inference, not routing. abs stays primary.

gateDesignLesson.lesson: Gate on what the grader ACTUALLY measures for the defect class. The text-collision-dedupe fix (removes user-#4 doubled labels) is human-visible but the grader's collisionRate skips same-text overlaps by design -> gating on collisionRate-drop wrongly reverted a correct fix. For human-visible-but-ungraded fixes, gate on the human-visible criterion (dupLabelsGone) + composite-no-reg + self-test, NOT a metric that cannot move.

coverageCrushConfirmed.finding: PROVEN (reactdev /tmp/pe-gsec-reactdev): the per-element score multiplies EVERY sub-score (color/typo/pos/text/effects) by areaCoverage, so on incomplete clones (low coverage) accurate sub-scores are FALSE-DEFLATED. reactdev raw color 0.8276 -> reported 0.16 via x0.1931. The grader conflates ACCURACY (high) with COMPLETENESS (low: 82/205 matched). The corpus is MORE accurate than the composite suggests; the gap is COMPLETENESS. Fix = #6 grader re-attribution (separate coverage dim + raw per-pair sub-scores), keeping coverage as its own penalized dimension (anti-gaming).

completenessIsTheLever.finding: CONFIRMED across #6: per-element ACCURACY is high (~0.85); the composite gap is COMPLETENESS (coverage). The grader's accuracy x coverage IS honest overall-fidelity (do NOT un-gate — gameable). To raise the composite: RAISE COVERAGE (match more of the source's real content), which the multiply amplifies. Two sub-levers: (a) capture genuinely-missing content (dynamic imagery/sections), (b) reduce OVER-COUNTED source nodes (decorative SVG/fragments inflating srcNodes) via fragment/section merge. Diagnose which dominates per site.

measurementIntegrityBug.finding: Cross-round corpus numbers are NOT comparable because (a) flow-router-rebaseline overwrote all 7 pages with FLOW builds and only some were rebuilt abs since -> a grade reads whatever builder last wrote the page; (b) grade-responsive RLG re-captures live + varies. PROOF: #6 graded reactdev at coverage 0.19/composite 0.53 (a FLOW leftover) while a fresh abs rebuild graded 0.96/0.616. FIX: a definitive clean re-baseline (rebuild ALL 7 with build-absolute, all recipes live, MULTI-PASS grade averaged) to reset to a known consistent state + trustworthy mean. Going forward, always rebuild-then-grade in the SAME round (never grade a page another round may have overwritten).

chunkedMediaNondeterminism.finding: tailwind grade spread 0.039 (vs ~0.001 elsewhere) traces to the chunked-media detector's chunkedMediaCount oscillating 2<->4 across identical-page captures -> struct 0.612<->0.756. Minor (only tailwind), but the chunked-media detector is capture-variance-sensitive. Low priority; median-of-3 absorbs it.

gateCalibrationLesson2.lesson: 2nd mis-calibrated gate this session (1st: text-collision v1 gated on an unmovable metric). dynamic-content-emit gated on structOn>structOff+0.02; the fix raised composite +0.042 + edit +0.07 + coverage +0.05 + bands-native + no-reg + self-test 1.0 but struct rose only 0.019 -> auto-restored a clear win. RULE: gate keeps on the HEADLINE objective (composite-up OR the user-principle the round serves) + no-reg + self-test; never let an arbitrary sub-score threshold veto a composite win.

directions: 1. REFINE-TO-1:1 inner loop (per-clone render->diff->repair->re-render until pixel-matched, then distill repairs into recipes) — raises the ceiling on every site; the biggest flywheel upgrade. refine.mjs exists (tasks #16/#25 completed) but likely stale vs the current abs pipeline + honest grader -> assess+rewire+prove+extend.

2. CAPTURE RESPONSIVE (multi-viewport 390/768/1440 capture + hybrid grid reflow) — the only path off the capped responsive floor (0.258); 1:1 requires matching the source's ACTUAL mobile layout we don't currently capture.

3. CORPUS BREADTH (20-archetype taxonomy: ecommerce/blog/portfolio/docs/dashboard/agency/restaurant) + DETERMINISTIC SYNTHETIC BENCH (hero/card-grid/nav/pricing/footer ground-truth) — makes wins generalize + kills measurement variance (half this session's reverts).

4. EDITABILITY QUALITY (global colors/fonts via Elementor variables, semantic nesting, a real editability metric) + MOTION capture (scroll/hover/parallax via the scoped GSAP/Pro-Motion escape hatch) — the weakest halves of '1:1 + editable the way a human dev would'.
META: parallelize the flywheel into worktree tracks (grader/capture/build/research touch different files) to 3-4x throughput.
phasePlan_2026_06_05.sequence: Start #1 (refine-to-1:1, highest ceiling-raise) + #3 (bench, for trustworthy generalization), then #2 (responsive), then #4. User selected ALL FOUR.

refineEngineBuilt.fact: refine-clone.mjs (per-clone refine driver) is built + proven: grade -> /tmp/pe-pairs per-pair deltas -> resolve widget id by text-Dice+box -> joist POST /pages/{id}/patch {ops:[{op:update_settings,element_id,settings}],expected_hash} -> re-grade -> keep-if-up (KEEP_EPS 0.004) -> clean revert. Live 5-widget/iter patching is below-noise; the engine is for LIVE position-nudge later. The per-pair repair-map output (perelement-score --emit-pairs / REFINE_PAIRS=1 -> /tmp/pe-pairs-<tag>.json) is additive, self-test 1.0.

buildTimeAccuracyRedirect.finding: The scalable per-element lever is BUILD-TIME accuracy snapping across ALL widgets, not live patches. nativeWidgetsHeightCost.note: Native-widget promotion (heading/button/image) achieves all editability deliverables (25 real h-tags, native buttons/images, globals propagate, editability 0.91) BUT causes a REAL 6.057x page-height blow-up (BOTH grade-sections AND grade-structure score ~0.47) because native IMAGE widgets bypass recipe #33s captured-size image-cap. Correctly reverted twice. VIABLE only after extending the image-cap to native image widgets. My grader-mismatch hypothesis was disproven by grading with the baseline grade-sections. Lesson: disambiguate a suspected grader-mismatch by RE-GRADING with the exact baseline grader before assuming a measurement bug.

compositeCeilingConfirmed.note: CONCLUSIVE: the supabase composite ceiling is ~0.574-0.581 under the CURRENT (false-deflating) grader, and NO autonomous build lever moves it. Proven by 4 consecutive composite-targeting levers all landing ~0.57: responsive RAM-grid #35 (flat), semantic-widgets (flat), native-widgets-composite #38 (neutral 0.574), height-tighten (flat 0.573). The cap is: visual pe 0.31 (coverage false-deflation, supervised) + responsive edgeSet/coverage (false-deflation, supervised) + hRatio ~1.95 (largely a SOURCE-headless lazy/virtualized-collapse artifact -> source appears ~7180px when it renders fuller live, so the clone-vs-source-headless ratio overstates over-tallness; not a fixable clone defect). The ONE thing that moves the composite is the SUPERVISED grader keystone (rasterized-area attribution / un-conflation, one line from working). AUTONOMOUS COMPOSITE SPACE EXHAUSTED. EDITABILITY built out (#37 globals + #38 native widgets). Holding (evidence-based, not premature) for the supervised decisions.
